title: Sales Development Representative (SDR) / Appointment Setter
description:
About Leda

We make LEDA, a learning experience platform that makes leadership development easy! We use expert interviews, animations, games, self-assessments and practical activities to turn managers into better leaders.

We work with CEOs and People & Culture leaders across Australia and APAC to help them develop the many people in their organisations. Our mission? Better organisations, by making it easy for companies to develop their established and emerging leaders. High quality and fun leadership development for all!

We're a small, profitable startup based in Melbourne. We like to laugh and have fun. And yes — you'll get leadership training too.



The Role

We're looking for someone to join our small but growing team with an SDR background wanting an accelerated pathway to Inside Sales.

This is a rare opportunity to become a critical member of a profitable start-up that has a real and positive impact on people's lives.



What You'll Be Doing



Converting inbound leads from our campaigns into conversations with the founders and yourself

Calling warmed leads (40–50 calls per day)

Nurturing and growing new business opportunities within the SMB segment (companies with <600 employees) in APAC

Understanding prospective customers' pain points and educating them on Leda's value

Guiding prospects through the sales process with the founder

Managing some opportunities to close, readying yourself to move into an Inside Sales role



Skills You'll Need



Minimum 12 months experience in an SDR or Inside Sales role

Highly empathetic and likeable on the phone, with great communication skills to explain, influence and persuade

Clear English with confident, friendly delivery

Preferably experience in tech, training, online learning, or selling to HR / People and Culture teams

Familiarity with Australian business culture is a plus





Hours & Pay

Schedule:



Part-time to start: 20 hours/week (Mon–Fri, 10am–12pm and 1–3pm AEST)

Path to full-time for top performers



Compensation:



$7 USD/hour base

$40 USD per sat meeting

Part-time earnings: ~$680–780/month

Full-time performers: $1,400–1,600+/month



Technical Requirements:



Your own computer or laptop (not a phone or tablet)

Stable internet connection (minimum 10 Mbps)

Headset with microphone

Quiet workspace with minimal background noise



Prove you can deliver, and we'll give you more hours and a clear path to Inside Sales.



How to Apply



Fill in this form ( 
 )

 and we'll send you our short screening process (about 30–45 minutes). It involves a speaking assessment, sales simulations, and a short video recording.

You'll have 72 hours to complete it. No resume needed — just show us what you can do.

Thank you very much for your time and interest!

Team Leda

title: AI Native Full Stack Developer - CRM Platform Build + Agency Projects (German-Speaking a Plus)
description:
About This Role

I'm Kenji, founder of a Germany-based web agency. I build websites, handle SEO, run Google Ads, and deliver design work for small-to-medium businesses. I've recently taken on a larger contract: building and maintaining a modular B2B CRM and lead-management platform for the automotive industry.

I'm looking for one strong developer to co-build this with me. You won't be handed tasks and left alone, we'll be working side by side in the codebase, architecting together, and shipping together. I'm technical, I build with AI coding agents daily, and I need someone who can match that pace and push the project forward independently when needed.

This is a long-term engagement. You'll work full-time during the active build phase, then transition to ~20 hours/month for ongoing maintenance, updates, and feature additions.



The CRM Project (Primary Focus)

You'll be the one of the developers building a modular web application (PWA) for car dealerships. The platform covers the full lifecycle of vehicle acquisition, lead management, and sales operations.

Core Modules

1. Online Acquisition Station (Customer-Facing)

Guided multi-step flow: vehicle data entry ? photo uploads (structured slots) ? contact info ? automatic confirmation + case number. Leads feed directly into the CRM.

2. Team Inbox & Lead Management

Shared inbox where all open leads are visible. Claim-to-lock system (one owner per lead, others can comment). Close with win/loss reason for clean KPI tracking.

3. Automations & Task Engine

Auto-generated tasks on new leads (e.g., "contact within 60 min"). Escalation rules: 24h inactivity ? warning, 48h ? lead returns to queue. Admin-configurable rule engine.

4. Dashboards & Reporting

User view: own leads, overdue follow-ups, appointments. Admin view: unhandled leads >24h, team workload, funnel metrics, close rates.

5. Listing Generator

Smart checklist for vehicle features ? auto-generated ad copy in multiple formats (premium, social, WhatsApp-short). Copy-button export for MVP, API sync later.

6. Document Generation

Auto-generated PDF templates (offers, reservations, draft contracts) with internal approval workflow. Clearly marked as drafts until finalized.

Future Phases (Add-ons)



Chat-based UI guide — button-driven flows for customers and internal team, with upgrade path to voice/video avatar

WhatsApp Business API integration

Outlook/M365 calendar sync via Microsoft Graph

DAT/VIN vehicle data lookup

Virtual test drive (walkaround video + live video scheduling)

Multi-location & role-based access



Tech Stack



Frontend: React / Next.js

Backend/DB: Supabase (or similar BaaS)

AI tooling: You'll be expected to use Claude Code, Codex, or similar agentic coding tools as your primary development workflow





What We're Looking For

Must-Have



Strong full-stack development skills — React/Next.js or other frontend, Supabase or similar backend, comfortable with APIs and integrations

Deep experience with AI coding agents — You don't just "use ChatGPT sometimes." You architect solutions with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or equivalent. You understand prompt engineering at a systems level, you know when to trust the agent and when to override it, and you can debug agent-generated code efficiently

Systems-level thinking — You can take a set of business requirements (like the modules above) and break them into clean architecture, data models, and implementation steps without being told how

Strong problem-solving ability — You've solved hard technical problems before, and you can describe exactly how

Clear English communication — Written and spoken

Git/GitHub proficiency

Reliable setup — Fast internet, good laptop, professional working environment

CET overlap — At least 4 hours of overlap with Central European Time for sync



Strong Signals We Value



You've built something ambitious on your own (app, tool, automation system, SaaS)

You've taught yourself hard skills without formal instruction

You've won or placed in competitions (programming, math, robotics, etc.)

You have personal projects that show depth (home lab, custom tooling, open-source contributions)

You've done freelance or contract work above what's typical for your experience level

You can explain complex technical decisions clearly and concisely



Big Plus: German Language Skills

Our clients and end-users are in the German market. If you speak or understand German, even at a conversational level, that's a significant advantage. It means you can read client requirements, understand product context, and potentially interact with stakeholders directly. Not a hard requirement, but it will put you ahead of other candidates.



Nice-to-Have



Experience with PWA development

WebSocket or Supabase Realtime experience

Experience with tools like Puppeteer, React-PDF, or PDFKit

Background job / queue handling (for escalation rules and auto-task engine)

API integration experience

Experience with Compliance (DSGVO / GDPR)





Work Structure



Build Phase: Full-time (~40 hours/week) until the platform is live and stable

Maintenance Phase: ~20 hours/month for updates, bug fixes, feature additions, and support

Additional agency work available between or alongside CRM phases





Compensation



60,000–100,000 PHP/month (depending on skill level) during the full-time build phase

Maintenance-phase compensation adjusted proportionally to hours

Performance-based increases as the engagement grows

Paid trial period to ensure mutual fit





How to Apply

Subject line: CRM Developer - AI-Native Full-Stack

kishiharahandel [at] gm ail com

In your application, include:

1. About You

Name, age, location, current work situation.

2. Proof of Ability

Share 2–3 concrete examples that demonstrate your capability. These could be:



A project you built end-to-end (what, why, how, and what was hard)

A technical problem you solved that required deep thinking

A skill you learned independently



Be specific. "I built websites" tells us nothing. "I built a real-time inventory sync between Shopify and a custom warehouse dashboard using Supabase edge functions and webhooks" tells us everything.

3. Your Hardest Technical Problem

In 5–10 sentences: What is the hardest technical problem you've ever solved? Walk us through your exact approach, including at least one specific technical detail.

4. Your AI Workflow

Describe how you currently use AI coding tools in your development process. Which tools, how you prompt them, what you trust them for, and where you don't.

5. Portfolio / Proof of Work

Links to anything you've built: GitHub repos, live sites, tools, automations, screenshots, videos, documentation. Non-technical achievements that show discipline and ability are also welcome.





This role requires someone who can think independently, solve novel problems, and build production-quality systems with modern AI-assisted workflows. If that describes you and this post excited you rather than intimidated you, we'd like to hear from you.

title: Sales Administration Support
description:
Work remotely | Full Time | Full training provided |



We’re hiring a high-output, high performing, natural admin talent to join the team as Sales Administration Support to be our admin and operations help behind our busy, high performing sales team.



Dale Carnegie is one of the world’s most recognised names in professional training, helping people and businesses unlock their potential in over 90 countries. We’re now looking for a highly organised, tech-savvy Virtual Assistant who can work at a fast pace, think ahead, and bring a keen eye for detail to everything they do.



This role is perfect for someone who’s resourceful, eager to learn, and excited by variety. You’ll have the opportunity to get to know our world-class training programs and understand our clients’ needs.



What You’ll Do:

You’ll work closely with our sales team, supervised by an experienced Sales Support & Ops Administrator, across a wide range of administration tasks to support both our government and commercial clients including:

- Client Database & CRM Management

- Surveys & Reporting

• Provide daily summary reports of survey responses.

• Run engagement reports



-
 Marketing

• Work with the Partnerships Manager to draft
s

• Schedule marketing
s aligned with upcoming events.

• Generate and file click/open performance reports consistently.

• Maintain a clear and simple campaign log.



- Event Setup & Monitoring (Eventbrite – In-House Projects)

• Set up events in Eventbrite.

• Monitor registrations and provide timely reports, including updates to the client lead database.



- Administrative Support for Pipeline Activities

Non Negotiables:

 marketing

Why You’ll Love Working With Us:

- Opportunity to grow as our business grows



If you’re ready to bring your admin and organisational skills, great attitude, and problem-solving mindset to a role where no two days are the same, we’d love to hear from you.



Apply now and help us deliver life-changing training experiences to clients across Queensland Australia.



How to Apply

Please submit:

title: Full-Time Real Estate Bookkeeping Assistant (Long-Term | Direct Hire)
description:
I am hiring a full-time bookkeeping assistant with real bookkeeping experience and real-estate accounting knowledge to support ongoing bookkeeping for multiple entities and properties.



You will be supporting a U.S.-based bookkeeping and operations firm that primarily serves real estate businesses, including rental and investing entities.



This is NOT a general VA role.

This is a bookkeeping role for someone who already understands how real-estate transactions should be treated in accounting (rentals, short-term rentals, and/or real-estate investing). If your experience is only training/certifications (and not real client work), please do not apply.



You do not need to know my exact software yet.

I am willing to train the right person on systems.



What I am not willing to train is basic bookkeeping or real-estate accounting concepts.



Primary Responsibilities

- Recording and categorizing real-estate transactions in QuickBooks Online (QBO)

- Supporting bookkeeping for:

  - long-term rentals (LTR)

  - short-term rentals (STR)

  - and real-estate investing activities

- Reconciling bank and credit card accounts

- Property-level and entity-level bookkeeping

- Preparing transactions for review and month-end close

- Assisting with real-estate closing and settlement entries (training provided)

- Working across multiple entities and properties



Required Experience (Non-Negotiable)



Please do NOT apply unless you meet all of the following:

- Real bookkeeping experience (not data entry and not administrative work)

- Real-estate bookkeeping experience or real-estate accounting knowledge, such as:

  - rental bookkeeping (LTR, STR, and/or MTR), and/or

  - real-estate investing or deal accounting

- QuickBooks Online (QBO) experience is required

- Strong understanding of how to properly categorize real-estate transactions

- Comfortable working with multiple properties and multiple entities



Preferred (Not Required – I Can Train)

- Experience with property management or real-estate software such as:

  - Buildium

  - AppFolio

  - REI Hub

  - or similar platforms

- Property management bookkeeping experience

- Experience supporting multiple properties or portfolios

Specific software experience is a preference, not a requirement.



Schedule & Availability

- Full-time role

 -Must be available to work either:

  - 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST, or

  - 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM EST

This is intended to be a long-term position



Compensation

- $4–$5 per hour during the first 6 months of onboarding and training

- Pay review after the initial training period based on performance and responsibility

- Paid consistently

- Long-term growth opportunity



What I’m Looking For

- Someone who wants a stable, long-term role

- Strong attention to detail

- Clear communication

- Able to follow established processes

- Comfortable asking questions instead of guessing

- Reliable internet and consistent availability



What I Do NOT Want

- Agencies

- General virtual assistants without bookkeeping experience

- Entry-level bookkeeping trainees

- Applicants juggling many full-time clients

- Short-term or “in-between” workers



HOW TO APPLY (IMPORTANT – READ CAREFULLY)



To be considered, you must answer the questions below.



Applications without complete answers will be ignored.

1. Describe your real-estate bookkeeping experience or real-estate accounting work in detail.

2. Describe your QuickBooks Online experience (how long and what type of work you performed).

3. What types of real-estate activities have you supported? (LTR, STR, MTR, investing, flips, etc.)

4. Are you available to work 9:00 AM–5:00 PM or 10:00 AM–6:00 PM EST? (Yes/No – specify which schedule.)

5. Why are you looking for a long-term role?

6. Do you have reliable and consistent internet suitable for full-time remote work?

7. Are you willing to work occasional overtime or weekends when needed? (for example, during year-end bookkeeping close-out or other urgent deadlines)?



As a test of attention to detail, please begin your application with:

I have real-estate bookkeeping experience and QBO experience.

? Real estate bookkeeping experience

? Experience with short-term rental income

- QuickBooks Online (QBO)

- REI Hub

- Buildium

- AppFolio

? Comfortable switching between different systems and clients



Preferred (Major Advantage)

Property management bookkeeping experience

Experience with multiple properties and entities



Schedule & Availability

 -MUST be available 8:00 AM – 12:00 PM EST during the training/onboarding period

- After onboarding, hours may be adjusted by mutual agreement

- This role can grow into full-time for the right person



Compensation

- Pay is based on experience

- Paid consistently (weekly or biweekly)

- Long-term opportunity for the right candidate



What I’m Looking For

- Someone who wants stable, long-term work

- Strong attention to detail

- Clear communication

- Able to follow established processes

- Reliable internet and consistent availability



What I Do NOT Want

? Agencies

? General VAs without real estate bookkeeping experience

? Applicants with many full-time clients

? Short-term or “in-between” workers



HOW TO APPLY (IMPORTANT)

- Which real estate or property management software have you used?

- Have you entered HUD statements before? If yes, explain

- Are you available 8am–12pm EST during onboarding? (Yes/No)

- Why are you looking for a long-term role?



As a test of your attention to detail, please start your application with the words:

“I have real estate bookkeeping experience.”

title: HR Admin Assistant
description:
Work Schedule: Monday to Friday, 8am - 5pm Pacific Time

Work Setup: Fully Remote



Position Overview

We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented HR Administrative Assistant to support our growing remote workforce. This role focuses on onboarding support, HR documentation, timekeeping management, and employee engagement while maintaining professional communication with US-based clients and Filipino recruits.

The ideal candidate has strong HR admin experience, excellent communication skills, empathy balanced with professionalism, and the initiative to improve processes without constant supervision.



Key Responsibilities

a. HR Administration

• Assist with onboarding newly hired recruits, including documentation, orientation scheduling, and account setup coordination.

• Maintain and monitor daily time records, attendance, and PTO credits for 50+ and growing remote employees.

• Keep HR files, recruit records, and client documents accurate, updated, and organized.

• Ensure HR compliance with company policies and client requirements.



b. Employee & Client Support

• Conduct monthly or bi-monthly check-ins with recruits and provide structured feedback reports to clients.

• Respond to HR-related inquiries from recruits and clients in a timely and professional manner.

• Support employee engagement activities and help address concerns while maintaining policy compliance.



c. Coordination

• Work closely with recruiters, managers, and clients to ensure smooth onboarding and employee support.

• Assist in policy communication, reminders, and HR announcements.



Qualifications

Required

• At least 3+ years of experience in an HR Admin, HR Assistant, or similar role.

• Strong written and verbal English communication skills and comfortable speaking with US clients.

• Experience handling employee onboarding, documentation, and timekeeping.

• Highly organized with strong attention to detail.

• Ability to balance empathy with policy enforcement.

• Self-starter with initiative and problem-solving mindset.

• Experience working remotely with distributed teams.



Preferred

• Experience in a recruitment/outsourcing environment.

• Familiarity with HRIS

• Basic knowledge of Philippine labor practices and remote work policies.

• Experience supporting 50+ employees.

title: Website / Landing Page / Pipeline Expert
description:

dical education.

etings.

.

title: Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable Specialist
description:
***Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable Specialist***

Company: Infinity22

Location: Remote

Employment Type: Full-Time



About Infinity22

Infinity22 is a fast-growing advisory, tax, and corporate finance firm operating across Australia, the UK, and the UAE. We work on high-value engagements spanning valuations, R&D tax incentives, capital raises, corporate restructuring, and strategic advisory. Our multidisciplinary team delivers insight, precision, and measurable impact to clients across diverse sectors. As we continue our expansion, we are strengthening our internal finance function with professionals who uphold the same high standards we deliver to our clients.



Role Overview

We are seeking an experienced Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable Specialist to support the financial operations of our global practice. This role is critical in ensuring timely and accurate processing of financial transactions, maintaining data integrity, and supporting the overall performance of the finance team. The ideal candidate brings strong technical capability, a commitment to accuracy, and the professionalism required to support a high-performance advisory firm.



Key Responsibilities

-Process accounts payable invoices, vendor payments, and expense claims in line with internal policies and compliance requirements.

-Manage accounts receivable activities including client invoicing, payment allocation, reconciliations, and follow-up of outstanding balances.

-Maintain accurate, organized, and audit-ready financial records across multiple jurisdictions.

-Prepare and analyze aging reports, identifying issues and escalating as needed.

-Assist with month-end closing, reconciliations, and reporting activities.

-Liaise with internal stakeholders across Australia, the UK, and the UAE to ensure accuracy and consistency in financial data.

-Support the finance team with process improvements and system enhancements as the firm continues to scale.



Qualifications & Experience

-Minimum 2 years of experience in accounts receivable and accounts payable roles.

-Proficiency with accounting software (e.g., Xero, QuickBooks, NetSuite, or similar).

-Strong Excel capabilities, including reporting and reconciliation skills.

-Sound understanding of accounting principles and financial controls.

-Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate across teams and regions.

-High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-growing environment.



What We Offer

-Competitive remuneration aligned with experience and performance.

-Exposure to a dynamic, international, and high-growth professional services environment.

-Opportunities for career development within a rapidly expanding firm.

-A collaborative culture built on trust, expertise, and continuous improvement.

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer (Next.js / React)
description:
We're a US-based insurtech startup helping consumers compare insurance and save money. Our MVP is live and we're looking for a senior developer to join our small founding team in Bohol and build out the full product.

This is not a BPO or outsourcing role. You'll work directly with the founder, make real architecture decisions, and build the core product. If you want to be an early employee at a startup rather than a cog in an agency, this is for you.



WHAT YOU'LL BUILD:

Insurance quote comparison engine with carrier API integrations

User onboarding and policy management flows

Cross-platform mobile app (iOS + Android via Capacitor)

Push notifications, real-time alerts, and user dashboard

Payment processing and checkout flows



TECH STACK:



Next.js, React, TypeScript

Capacitor (iOS and Android builds)

PostgreSQL

REST APIs / third-party integrations

AWS or Vercel



REQUIREMENTS:



You've shipped production apps used by real customers — not just tutorials and side projects

Deep expertise in Next.js and React (server components, app router, SSR/SSG, API routes — you know the internals, not just the docs)

You've built and deployed mobile apps live in the App Store / Google Play

You can design a database schema, write efficient queries, and optimize for scale

You've integrated third-party APIs in production and dealt with the edge cases

Strong quantitative background — comfortable with statistics, probability, data modeling. CS + Math/Stats types thrive here.

You can look at a dataset and think about what to build with it, not just how to display it

You can take a rough idea and turn it into a working feature by end of day

Based in Bohol OR willing to relocate (relocation support available)

Strong English communication



BONUS:



Background in insurance, fintech, actuarial science, or risk modeling

You've built pricing engines, recommendation systems, or decision algorithms

Early-stage startup experience

CI/CD, monitoring, alerting from scratch

AI/ML experience — understanding the math, not just calling APIs

GitHub or portfolio that speaks for itself



Start your application with the word "lighthouse" so we know you read this.

title: Full-Time Video Editor – Automotive Content
description:
We’re a U.S.-based marketing agency that works with car dealerships and auto brands across the West Coast. Right now, we’re looking for a skilled video editor to help us with a batch of upcoming commercial projects.



This is a freelance, project-based role to start, but there’s strong potential for it to turn into a long-term position if it’s the right fit.



What You'll Be Working On:

Editing short-form horizontal ad content (mainly 30-second commercials for TV and streaming platforms)



Adding motion graphics, legal text, pricing overlays, and branded callouts



Making sure everything feels polished, on-brand, and ready for broadcast or digital use



Collaborating with our creative team (we’ll give you a clear vision, script, and assets to work with)



What We're Looking For:

Solid editing skills in CapCut (we use this for most basic edits)



Advanced ability in Adobe Premiere Pro and After Effects (especially for motion graphics)



Experience editing horizontal video content for ads — doesn’t have to be on actual TV, but should look like it could be



Strong sense of pacing, clean design, and storytelling



Bonus if you’ve worked with automotive content, or are familiar with U.S. ad standards



Schedule:

Must be available Tuesday through Saturday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM PH time



Occasional coordination with our U.S. team (Pacific Time overlap)



Trial Task:

If you’re shortlisted, we’ll ask you to complete a quick paid trial — it’s just a way for us to see how you work with creative direction and motion graphics.



To Apply:

Please include:



A short intro about yourself and your background



A portfolio link showing TV-style or branded horizontal ad content



Your experience level with CapCut, Premiere, and After Effects



Confirmation that you’re available during the hours listed above



When you’re able to start



Just to make sure you’re actually reading this — please include the words “Zoom-Zoom” in your application.

title: Part-Time Cold Caller — US Small Business Outreach
description:
North Shore Labs is a web design agency based in Chicago. We’re looking for a part-time cold caller to reach out to small businesses and book walkthrough appointments with our strategist.



What you’ll be doing:



Calling US small businesses from a provided lead list. You’re offering a free Competitor Intelligence Brief: a report we put together showing what the top businesses in their area are doing online. If they’re interested, you book a 10-minute walkthrough call with our team. That’s it. You’re offering something free and booking a time.



Hours:

Roughly 4 hours per day during US Central Time business hours (9am-1pm CT or 1pm-5pm CT). This means night shift Philippine time. You must be comfortable and reliable working these hours.



What we’re looking for:



Clear, natural-sounding English. You need to sound like a person sharing something interesting, not a person reading a script. 

Resourceful — if you hit a receptionist, you figure out how to reach the owner.

Reliable — you show up on time and hit your dials. Prior cold calling experience is a plus but not required if you’re a strong communicator.

title: B2B Lead Generation Specialist  Cold Email and Linkedin (Smartleads, Apollo and SalesNavigator)
description:
We're a small people skills training company looking for a sharp execution-focused lead gen specialist to run cold
 and LinkedIn outreach campaigns on behalf of our team. You'll work directly with the founder and be given clear strategy, ICPs, and messaging, we have infrastructure build, your job is to manage it, suggest improvements, run the sequences, and keep the machine humming.



What You'll Actually Do

Build and manage cold
 campaigns in Smartlead (sequences, inbox rotation, deliverability monitoring)

Initially source prospect lists using our tools. 

Set up and manage LinkedIn outreach sequences (connection requests, follow-ups)

Monitor campaign performance and report on reply rates, bounce rates, more info and booked meetings

Flag issues proactively and suggest optimisations, don't wait to be asked





Must-Haves

Hands-on experience with Smartlead, Apollo, and Sales Navigator, not just "familiar with"

Understanding of
 deliverability (SPF, DKIM, DMARC, domain warm-up)

Strong written English, you'll occasionally need to personalise copy at scale

Reliable, fast communicator who follows briefs closely



Nice to Have

Exposure to Australian or US B2B markets





You're a fit if you:

Take ownership, QA your own work, and treat a missed send or a broken sequence as something that matters.

title: Print Production Graphic Designer – Reusable Bags (Part-Time / Ongoing)
description:
Part-time / Freelance

Long-term, ongoing work



About the Role:

We are looking for an experienced print-production graphic designer to prepare factory-ready artwork for reusable shopping bags supplied to major U.S. retailers. This role is production-focused, not social media or branding design.



You will work primarily in Adobe Illustrator, converting approved designs into accurate print files for woven and non-woven polypropylene bags.



Key Responsibilities:



Prepare print-ready Illustrator files for NWPP and WPP bags



Build artwork on factory die lines and templates



Manage front, back, gussets, bottom panels, seams, and safe zones



Set up spot colors (Pantone), bleeds, traps, and repeat lengths



Revise artwork efficiently based on factory feedback



Maintain clean layers, file naming, and version control



Required Experience:



Strong experience with flexographic and/or gravure printing



Hands-on work with woven or non-woven polypropylene packaging or bags



Understanding of print tolerances, seam loss, and registration shift



Ability to deliver press-ready files with minimal supervision



Comfortable working with overseas factories and technical feedback



Required Skills:



Adobe Illustrator (expert level)



Print production and prepress setup



Packaging or industrial print experience



Not a Fit If You Primarily Do:



Social media graphics



Web or UI design



Logo-only branding work



What We Offer:



Clear direction and templates



Consistent, repeat work



Fast turnaround projects



Long-term relationship for the right person



To Apply:

Please include examples of production or packaging work (AI or PDF). Mockups alone are not sufficient.

description:
About the Role



We’re looking for an Admin Lead to take ownership of administrative operations, improve systems and workflows, and run our VA Business and Internship Program.



This is more than a task-based role — it’s about taking initiative, building processes, using AI/tools to work smarter, and driving measurable results.

title: Growth & Ads Specialist – Google Ads, SEO & Digital Experiments (Remote, Part-Time) (German Language is a Plus)
description:
The situation

I run a small web agency serving German SMBs, trades, hospitality, local service providers. One of my clients is in an active growth phase: they've got a solid foundation and are now trying to expand their customer base, increase visibility, and figure out which channels actually move the needle for their business.

I'm not looking for someone to just run ads. I'm looking for someone who can look at a business in early growth, ask the right questions, and figure out what's actually worth trying, then execute it, measure it, and iterate.



What this actually involves



Optimizing Google Ads campaigns, maintaining and optimizing the campaign, fixing any issues (Search as the core, potentially expanding from there)

Basic local SEO groundwork, on-page fundamentals, making sure the basics aren't broken

Thinking beyond the obvious: if something isn't working, you have ideas about why and what to try next

Communicating results clearly, what's working, what isn't, what we should do about it, without drowning the client in jargon

Being honest about what's realistic for a small business with a limited budget





What I'm actually looking for

The technical skills matter, but they're table stakes. What I care more about is how you think.

This client is early in their growth journey. That means there's no established playbook yet, we're figuring out what works for their specific market, their specific audience, their specific budget. I need someone who is comfortable with that ambiguity, finds it interesting rather than frustrating, and brings genuine curiosity to the problem.

You should be able to:



- Look at a business and quickly identify where the low-hanging fruit is

- Set up a campaign with a clear hypothesis, not just "let's see what happens"

- Kill things that aren't working without getting attached to them

- Spot opportunities that aren't in the brief



You don't need a formal background in marketing. You do need to have actually done this before and have something concrete to show for it.





Nice to have



- Experience with small or local businesses specifically (big brand experience doesn't always transfer)

- GEO, AEO experience is a plus

- Familiarity with the German market or DACH consumer behavior, German language skills are a plus, even at a conversational level

- Any experience adjacent to growth:
, organic social, conversion optimization, landing page testing, the more tools in your kit, the better





To apply

The formal cv and cover letter is of course good too, but more importantly, send me:



One concrete example, a campaign or growth experiment you ran, what you were trying to achieve, what you tested, and what the outcome was. Numbers are great, but honest reflection matters more than impressive figures.

One question you'd ask before touching the ads account of a local business that's trying to grow, the question that would tell you the most about whether the strategy makes sense. Send me the application to:

Subject: Google Ads & SEO Growth Specialist

title: UX Researcher / Field Interviewer (Philippines, user interviews)
description:
Looking for a Philippines-based UX researcher who can recruit and interview local small business owners and summarize insights.



Hi! I’m looking for a Philippines?based UX researcher who can help me understand how small service businesses work (for example barbershops, salons, etc.) and turn that into clear, actionable insights for a new product.



You will be responsible for:

• Finding and recruiting up to 10 relevant participants in the Philippines (mainly small business owners).

• Conducting 1:1 user interviews (online or offline).

• Asking structured but friendly, conversational questions (I’ll provide a starting guide; you can adapt it).

• Taking good notes and/or recording calls (with consent) / ask to make and make photos of the current tools or software, make sketches of manual stuff like notebooks, tables, lists, that owners use in the everyday work, etc.

• Writing short English summaries after each interview (key quotes, pains, workflows, patterns).

• Producing a brief synthesis after a batch of interviews (what’s common, what’s surprising, what we should test next).



Requirements

• Located in the Philippines (Metro Area) and comfortable talking to local small business owners.

• Spoken and written English.

• Communication with easy.

• Confident doing outreach and scheduling your own interviews.

• Reliable, responsive, and able to work independently with minimal supervision.

Deliverables / outcomes

• 10 completed interviews per week (2 hours per 1 interview).

• For each interview:

   • raw notes or transcript +

   • your English summary (context, main pains, interesting quotes).



Could be regular contract for another niches / researches

title: Funnel Builder
description:

- Access to the latest tools and technologies in digital marketing and e-commerce.

title: Sales & Tech Representative
description:
We’re Hiring!!!



Are you a skilled salesperson with a background in gas plumbing and/or electrical work? Do you enjoy helping customers find the right solution while using your technical knowledge? Join our growing team and work with a US online home goods retailer that values expertise, professionalism, and customer satisfaction.





Position:

Sales & Tech Representative – Technical Products (Gas & Electrical Focus)

Location: Remote (Philippines-based)

Schedule: Monday-Friday, 11:00 AM to 7:00 PM EST (40 hours/week)

Salary: Competitive + Performance Bonuses





About the Role:

As a Salesperson, you will assist customers with product selection, provide technical support during the sales process, and close sales with confidence. You’ll work with gas fireplaces, heaters, stoves, and other appliances, so previous hands-on experience or technical knowledge of gas or electrical systems is a MUST.





Job Duties and Responsibilities:

Receive incoming phone calls and live chats from potential customers (prospects) who indicated that they need shopping assistance. This does not include calls and chats from existing customers that need help with their orders (that’s a job for a different department)

Help customers choose the right gas or electric heating appliance for their needs

Makes follow-up calls, writes follow-up
s, and text messages to potential buyers. We try to follow up 5x with each person that contacted us (unless they indicate that they are no longer interested). We don’t do cold calling. Only reaching out to prospects who interacted with our online store and left their contact information.

After each interaction with a prospect/customer, writes notes & comments in our CRM (Asana)

Communicate efficiently and build rapport with potential customers

Assists prospects in their shopping journey and helps make the purchase decision

Ensures that all prospects’ questions and objections are addressed

Leads every interaction to a close sale, which means an order is placed on our online store

Prepares custom quotes, price matches competitors if necessary, and offers custom discounts. This requires calculating margins & ensuring profitability.

Answering questions about the products we offer, including technical questions. Learns the specifications, features and benefits of our bestsellers. Over time, becomes an expert in the industry.

Explain technical specifications and installation requirements in simple terms

If not able to answer product-specific questions, conducts research in the materials provided by the supplier, or reaches out to the supplier to get the information

Confirms stock availability and lead times when offering products to prospects

Documents standard procedures and best practices in a written form, so that other tea
mbers can learn from them





Requirements:

Sales Experience – 2+ years, preferably with B2C or B2B technical products

Gas Plumbing or Electrical Experience – Formal training or work experience (installer, technician, or similar roles preferred)

Excellent English – Both written and spoken

Confident Communicator – Can explain complex concepts to non-technical customers

Tech-Savvy – Comfortable using CRMs,
 platforms, order tools, etc.

Reliable and Proactive – Takes initiative and works independently

Home Office Setup – Stable internet, noise-free environment, backup power is a plus





Preferred but Not Required:

Knowledge of venting, HVAC, or hearth appliances

US product or code familiarity

Experience in the construction or home improvement industry





Why Join Us?

Sales commission: 8% of gross profit from all orders closed. Gross profit means the sale amount minus the product and shipping cost. Paid monthly. No upper limit. Monthly commission payouts range between $1,200 (the guaranteed minimum) and as high as $3,000 (for the top performer). The average monthly payout is $1,465 (average from the last 2 years).

$1,200/month minimum guaranteed pay

15 days paid days off per year (also convertible to min. guaranteed commission)

Paid training on all aspects of the role

Opportunities for professional development

Friendly atmosphere

Online Interview process

Fully remote position





How to Apply:

Apply through OnlineJobs.ph with the subject line: "Sales Pro - [Your Name]"

(BPO/call center outbound experience is a plus)



Proof (Required)

If you’ve done US outbound campaigns before, include: industry + daily call volume + results/KPIs.

How to Apply (Required)

Start your message with: APPOINTMENT

Upload a 30-second voice recording saying this exactly (sound natural):

“Hi, this is [your name]. I saw you may be hiring someone to help with calls or scheduling, and I wanted to ask a quick question about how you’re currently handling incoming calls when things get busy.”



Answer:

a) Highest calls you made in a day?

b) Last high-volume calling job + volume?

c) Would 100 calls/day feel normal or stressful?



No recording = not reviewed.

title: Content & Automation VA — AI Specialist
description:
About Lashed It

Lashed It is a premium lash extension education business based on the Gold Coast, Australia. We train women to become skilled, confident lash technicians and build profitable businesses from scratch. Our in-person courses are fully booked months in advance and backed by lifetime student support.

The founder actively uses AI tools in every part of the business. We are not looking for someone to learn AI on the job. We want someone who already lives and breathes it.

 

The Role

We are hiring a part time VA for approximately 10 hours per week to own our content creation and student communications. This is a remote, async role. You will be given clear direction and brand guidelines and expected to work independently without hand holding.

You will be the person behind the content that represents this brand. Quality, consistency, and attention to detail are non negotiable.

 

What You Will Be Doing

Content Creation

• Creating 3 Instagram Reels/Carousels per week

• Repurposing top performing Reels and carousels into Instagram Stories

• Using AI tools to speed up production without sacrificing quality

 

Student Support

• Monthly check ins with enrolled students via DM

• Keeping track of where students are in their journey and flagging anything that needs the founder's attention

 

Lead Follow Ups

• Weekly follow up with leads who have enquired but not yet enrolled using our existing scripts and sequences

 

Course Support

• Editing online course modules as directed (video cuts, captions, structure)

• Assisting with course creation tasks as needed



Automation

?Setting up and managing ManyChat flows for DM automation and lead capture

?Maintaining and updating automations as offers or messaging changes



Community

?Setting up a student community 

?Creating and scheduling regular posts to help students

?Moderating and managing the community on an ongoing basis



TikTok

?Repurposing existing Reels content for TikTok



What We Need From You

• Proven experience using AI tools for content creation (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar)

• Strong Canva skills and an eye for clean, premium design

• Experience with Instagram content strategy, not just scheduling

• Excellent written English and the ability to write in someone else's voice

• Self directed with strong communication — you flag issues, you do not wait to be chased and reach out for help

• Available for async work with a reasonable turnaround within AEST business hours

 

This Is Not The Right Role If

• You are still learning how to use AI tools

• You need detailed instructions for every task

• You treat content creation as just scheduling posts

• You have never worked with a personal brand or service business before

• You don’t have fast internet connection 



 

Details

• Hours: approximately 10 hours per week to start

• Rate: please include your hourly rate or weekly rate in your application

• Location: fully remote, must be comfortable working async

• Start date: as soon as the right person is found

 

How to Apply

Send an
 to 
 with the subject line: Lashed It VA Application

 

Your application must include:

• Start your message with the words Lashed It so we know you read this in full

• A short paragraph on how you use AI in your content workflow

• Two to three examples of Instagram content you have created for a client or yourself

• Your availability and rate

 

Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered.

title: Director of Operations-
description:
Director of Operations



Short Term Rental Portfolio- 22 units

Full Time (40 hours per week)



Role Overview



The Director of Operations is responsible for the overall operational performance, systems, and profitability of the short term rental portfolio. This role owns the operational infrastructure that allows the company to scale while maintaining high guest satisfaction and strong financial discipline.



The Director of Operations leads the operations team, oversees property performance, manages vendors, and ensures that systems and processes are continuously improved.



This role has direct oversight of Supervisors and Guest Relations Specialists and serves as the operational backbone of the company.



Core Responsibilities

Leadership and Team Oversight



Lead and manage the operations team to ensure properties run smoothly and guests receive a high quality experience.



Responsibilities include:



• Direct oversight of Level 2 Supervisors and Level 3 Guest Relations Specialists

• Hiring, interviewing, and onboarding new tea
mbers

• Making termination decisions when necessary

• Sending contracts and onboarding documentation to new hires

• Running a weekly 1 hour operations tea
eting

• Creating a culture of accountability and operational excellence



Financial Control and Expense Oversight



Maintain financial discipline across the portfolio and ensure operational spending aligns with company profitability goals.



Responsibilities include:



• Approving invoices over $150

• Paying maintenance vendors and contractors

• Monitoring operational spending across properties

• Providing cleaning performance and expense reports to finance twice per month

• Communicating with property owners regarding operational expenses and property performance



Systems and Process Architecture



Own and maintain the operational systems that allow the business to scale efficiently.



Responsibilities include:



• Maintaining and updating the company SOP library

• Identifying and closing operational gaps

• Improving internal workflows and operational processes

• Owning and maintaining the Asana project management structure

• Managing and optimizing Guesty property management software

• Managing and optimizing the Happy Guest system

• Creating training materials for tea
mbers

• Duplicating and assigning Asana templates when onboarding new properties



Strategic Operations Oversight



Ensure the operations team and systems are built for long term growth and operational excellence.



Responsibilities include:



• Establishing escalation governance for operational issues

• Designing scalable operational processes

• Ensuring operational readiness for portfolio expansion

• Setting and maintaining quality control standards across properties

• Continuously improving operational performance



Operational Accountability Structure



Level 3: Guest Relations Specialists

Responsible for guest communication, response speed, and guest satisfaction.



Level 2: Supervisors

Responsible for resolving operational issues, coordinating vendors, and overseeing day to day property operations.



Level 1: Director of Operations

Responsible for system design, financial control, team leadership, and overall portfolio profitability.

title: Shopify Ecommerce VA – European Car Enthusiast
description:
We are looking for a true car enthusiast to join our team as a Shopify Ecommerce VA.



This role is for someone who genuinely lives and breathes cars, especially European vehicles. You must have strong knowledge of European makes and models, parts, trims, generations, and general car culture. We are not looking for someone who is casually interested in cars or someone trying to fake automotive knowledge.



You should be comfortable working inside Shopify and helping with ecommerce-related tasks such as product organization, listings, collections, tagging, product research, and general store support.



Important: please do not apply unless you are a real enthusiast with hands-on knowledge of European cars. If selected for an interview, I will personally call you and ask detailed automotive questions to verify your knowledge. Do not use ChatGPT or fake experience to try to get through the application process.



We want someone honest, sharp, detail-oriented, and passionate about the automotive space.



Ideal Candidate

 & Instagram) and other relevant platforms. The ideal candidate has a balance of creativity and analytical skill — capable of writing compelling ad copy, analysing performance metrics, and continuously improving campaign outcomes.

title: Trading Assistant
description:
I am looking for a reliable trading assistant to help execute stock trades using Interactive Brokers (IBKR). This role is focused strictly on trade execution and monitoring, not strategy. All trades and instructions will be provided by me.



This position is ideal for someone who understands trading platforms and order types and can follow precise instructions carefully.



Responsibilities

 • Execute stock trades in Interactive Brokers based on instructions

 • Place and manage limit orders, stop losses, and trailing stops

 • Monitor positions and alerts during trading hours

 • Adjust orders when instructed

 • Maintain a simple trade log in Google Sheets or Excel

 • Notify me of filled orders or unusual activity



Important:

You will not be responsible for d
 which trades to make. Your role is strictly execution and monitoring.



Requirements

 • Basic understanding of stock trading

 • Familiarity with order types (limit, market, stop, trailing stop)

 • Experience with Interactive Brokers is a plus

 • Strong attention to detail

 • Reliable internet connection

 • Ability to follow precise instructions

 • Comfortable communicating quickly during market hours



Preferred

 • Experience using trading platforms such as IBKR, TradingView, ThinkorSwim, etc.

 • Experience assisting traders or working in financial markets

 • Basic spreadsheet skills (Excel / Google Sheets)



Work Schedule

Flexible, but availability during U.S. market hours is preferred.



Compensation

Hourly or fixed monthly compensation depending on experience.



To Apply

Please include:

 1. Your experience with trading platforms

 2. Whether you have used Interactive Brokers before

 3. Your time zone

 4. A short description of your experience with order types (limit, stop, trailing stop)



Looking forward to working with someone reliable and detail-oriented.

title: Meta Ads Media Buyer for Shopify Ecommerce – Purchase Campaigns Only
description:
Company:

CCAC Pet Essentials LLC

United States-registered ecommerce business



Job Type:

Remote | Part-Time to Start | Potential Long-Term Role



About Us

CCAC Pet Essentials is a U.S.-registered LLC operating a Shopify ecommerce store in the pet products space. We are looking for a skilled Meta Ads Media Buyer who understands how to drive real purchases, not just traffic, page likes, or vanity metrics.



We want a performance-focused person who has already handled ecommerce storefronts and can provide solid, verifiable proof of sales results from stores they previously managed.



What We Need

We are hiring a Meta Ads specialist who can manage and improve
 and Instagram purchase campaigns for our Shopify store. This role is focused on generating profitable sales through paid social using a structured testing and scaling approach.



Your Responsibilities

Launch, manage, and optimize Meta purchase campaigns for Shopify ecommerce

Conduct product, angle, audience, and creative testing

Build and optimize retargeting campaigns

Monitor key metrics including CTR, CPC, CPA, ROAS, AOV, and conversion rate

Review landing pages and product pages and suggest improvements that may increase conversions

Coordinate ad creative requests and recommend winning concepts

Provide clear weekly performance reports with actions, findings, and next steps

Help identify underperforming ads and scale profitable ones responsibly

Maintain proper attribution and tracking discipline



Required Experience

Proven experience running Meta Ads for Shopify ecommerce

Strong focus on purchase campaigns, not engagement or follower growth

Solid understanding of retargeting, funnel stages, and ecommerce performance metrics

Ability to read and explain campaign performance clearly

Experience testing creatives and scaling winning ads

Familiarity with Meta Ads Manager, Shopify, Pixel, and event tracking



Mandatory Proof Requirement

To be considered, you must provide verifiable proof of ecommerce results from stores or brands you previously handled. 



Please include:

Store niche or product category

Your exact role in the account

Time period managed

Ad spend handled

Results achieved, such as purchases, CPA, ROAS, or revenue growth

Screenshots from Ads Manager, Shopify, or reporting dashboards with sensitive information hidden as needed

At least 1–2 references, case studies, or storefront examples we can verify

Applications without proof of real ecommerce sales performance will not be considered.



Nice to Have

Experience in pet, home, lifestyle, or dropshipping/ecommerce brands

Experience with creative strategy

Familiarity with UGC-style ad concepts and direct-response ecommerce ads

Experience improving conversion paths, bundles, upsells, or offer positioning



What Success Looks Like

Improved purchase volume fro
ta campaigns

Lower cost per purchase over time

Profitable ROAS based on agreed targets

Consistent testing and reporting

Clear contribution to Shopify sales growth



Compensation

Base compensation based on experience. Please include your preferred rate and compensation model in your application

Plus 2%–4% commission on attributable Meta-driven sales, paid only after agreed tracking and minimum ROAS target are met. 

10–20 hours per week to start



Opportunity to move to monthly retainer after results are proven



How to Apply

Please send:

A short introduction

Your experience with Shopify ecommerce and Meta purchase campaigns

Screenshots or case studies showing verifiable sales results

The monthly ad budgets you have handled

Your preferred pay structure

Your availability and timezone



Important

We are not looking for followers, likes, boosted posts, or vanity metrics. We are only looking for someone who can help grow real ecommerce purchases in a measurable and professional way.

title: Sales Automation Manager (AI Systems & Lead Gen)
description:
Role Overview

As our Sales Automation Manager, you will be the "engine room" of our sales department. We are a robotics company leveraging cutting-edge technology, and we need a technical operator to manage our Marblism Sales AI ecosystem.



You won't be making cold calls; instead, you will be managing 3 AI sales agents, maintaining the outreach infrastructure (LinkedIn/Gmail), and ensuring our human sales team has a consistent flow of meetings in their Calendly.



Key Responsibilities

- AI Orchestration: Monitor and optimize 3 Marblism Sales AI agents to ensure high-quality outreach and engagement.



- Infrastructure Management: Set up and manage professional Gmail and LinkedIn accounts for the AI personas (ensuring they stay under sending limits to avoid being flagged).



- Lead Conversion: Monitor AI conversations and ensure interested prospects are successfully booked into Calendly.



- Sales Reporting: Daily updates to the sales tracking report. You are responsible for reporting the number of invites sent, replies received, and meetings booked.



- Deliverability Maintenance: Proactively manage "warm-up" tools for Gmail and LinkedIn to ensure our messages land in the primary inbox.



- Data Integrity: Ensure all lead data is accurately synced between the AI tools and our internal tracking sheets.



Technical Requirements

- Automation Experience: Proven experience with Sales Automation tools (e.g., Marblism, GHL, Apollo, or Instantly).



- Platform Mastery: Expert-level knowledge of LinkedIn (Sales Navigator) and Gmail (Workspace) account management.



- Metric-Driven: Comfortable working with spreadsheets and CRM data to provide clear, daily sales reports.



- Problem Solver: Ability to troubleshoot why an automation might have paused or why a lead didn't sync correctly.



- English Proficiency: High level of written English to "humanize" AI scripts when necessary.



Tools You Will Use

- AI: Marblism (Sales AI)



- Outreach: LinkedIn & Gmail



- Scheduling: Calendly



- Reporting: Google Sheets / CRM



- Communication: Slack/WhatsApp



How to Apply

Please submit your resume and a brief summary of your experience managing automated outreach campaigns to

title: Wedding Photographer VA & Photo Culler
description:
About the Role:

I’m looking for a detail-oriented, organized, and enthusiastic Virtual Assistant to help with photo culling for weddings. This role is perfect for someone who loves photography, has a great eye for storytelling, and can efficiently select the best images from full wedding galleries for editing. You will be responsible for reviewing large wedding photo collections and narrowing them down to a polished set of images that reflect the emotions, moments, and variety of the day.



Key Responsibilities:

-  Review full wedding galleries (typically 4,000–6,000 images per wedding) and select the best images for editing, narrowing them down to approximately 800–1,200 per wedding.

-  Maintain variety in selections, especially for key moments such as the ceremony, first dance, and couple portraits.

- Choose multiple options for important moments, including: Different facial expressions (smiles, laughs, natural reactions, tears); Different framing (full body vs close-up, portrait vs landscape orientation)

- Deliver selected images within 48 hours of receiving the gallery to allow for timely sneak peek deliveries to the couple.

- Use the provided photo culling software to efficiently select images; training guides and videos will be provided. Feedback will be given at the start to help you learn my style and preferences.



Qualifications & Skills:

- Strong attention to detail and excellent organizational skills

- Experience in photography, photo editing, or culling is highly preferred

- Ability to make aesthetic decisions that align with the style of the photographer

- Comfortable working with large sets of images efficiently

- Must have a reliable, high-speed internet connection capable of downloading thousands of high-resolution JPEG images

- Strong English communication skills and responsiveness

- Self-motivated, reliable, and able to work independently

title: PRODUCTION COORDINATOR SORORITY APPAREL
description:
PRODUCTION COORDINATOR

We're hiring an experienced Production Coordinator to manage the behind-the-scenes operations that keep our orders moving. This is a hands-on role that sits at the center of our supplier relationships, order flow, and production quality.

We specialize in custom apparel for college sororities and fraternities across the United States. Greek life organizations operate on tight, event-driven timelines — think Bid Day, recruitment week, formal season, and chapter events — where hundreds to thousands of garments need to be produced, approved, and delivered within strict windows. There is very little room for error, and the pace is unlike most apparel businesses.

This role is only open to candidates currently working or with direct past experience in the Greek apparel industry. If you have not worked with sorority or fraternity clients in a production, operations, or sourcing capacity, please do not apply.

Your day-to-day will include:



Communicating directly with suppliers and overseas factories

Placing, organizing, and tracking orders end-to-end

Sourcing materials and products based on client needs

Reviewing design files before production to ensure accuracy and quality

Organizing and packaging information for suppliers to minimize errors and delays

Coordinating approvals, revisions, timelines, and deadlines

Managing MOQs, lead times, and production limitations across multiple orders simultaneously



This role requires strong organizational skills, sharp attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple supplier relationships without things slipping through the cracks.

Compensation is based on experience.

title: Greek-Life Custom Apparel - Multiple Roles (Operations / Sales / Design)

-High attention to detail

-Comfortable in a fast-moving, high-expectation environment

Why Work With Us:



-Long-term role with a growing premium Greek apparel brand

-Clear systems and structured workflows

-Real opportunity to grow into leadership roles

-Work directly with a U.S.-based ownership team

-Competitive pay based on experience



HOW TO APPLY:

-Specific sorority/fraternity experience

-Samples of past work (mockups, brands, or role descriptions)

title: Legal Assistant
description:
We are seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Legal Assistant to provide day-to-day administrative support to our legal department. This role is ideal for someone who can manage multiple priorities independently while maintaining accuracy, confidentiality, and professionalism in a fast-paced legal environment.



Main Responsibilities

* Manage legal administrative tasks, including calendaring, document preparation, and record-keeping.

* Handle and respond to
 correspondence professionally and promptly.

* Communicate with courts, clients, and other external parties as required.

* Prepare, format, and organize legal documents with accuracy and attention to detail.

* Maintain confidential case files and ensure proper documentation procedures.

* Support attorneys and legal staff with administrative and ad hoc tasks.

* Assist with basic legal research and reporting when needed.

* Strong organizational skills with high accuracy and attention to detail.

* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

* Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion and integrity.

* Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks effectively.

* Comfortable collaborating with team members in a fast-paced environment.



How to Apply

If you are a dedicated legal professional who thrives in a fast-paced and collaborative environment, we would love to hear from you.



Please complete the application form here:?



You may also include a Google Drive link to your most recent resume (ensure the file is set to Anyone with the link can view) and include “Legal Assistant” in the subject line of your application.



All information shared will be treated confidentially and used solely for recruitment purposes.

Note: This is a private U.S. company — not a recruitment agency.

title: Hotel Sales Expert
description:
Job Summary



We are seeking a proactive, results-driven Sales Manager to drive revenue and mine new sales opportunities our 100-room hotel in Alachua, FL. You will do a combination of identifying new business opportunities as well as following up on existing leads.  Your goal is to maximize occupancy and RevPAR for the hotel.



Core Responsibilities

• Lead Generation & Prospecting: Actively research and solicit new business through cold calling,
 campaigns, and monitoring local Alachua/Gainesville area events (University of Florida events, youth sports tournaments, and local corporate projects).

• Inquiry Management: Respond promptly to all Sales leads via phone or
 as needed and follow up with all leads on a regular basis.

• Account Management: Build and maintain relationships with local businesses, travel managers, and tour operators to secure repeat "LNR" (Local Negotiated Rate) business.

• Market Analysis: Monitor competitor rates and local market trends to suggest strategic pricing adjustments to the General Manager.

• Reporting: Provide weekly sales activity reports, including "Calls Made," "Groups Booked," and "Revenue Pipelined."

 

Candidate Requirements

Experience & Skills

• Hospitality Experience: Minimum 1–2 years in hotel sales or front office operations 

• Availability: Avaialbe to work during business hours in the US.  9am – 5pm Eastern Time.

• Sales Acumen: Proven ability to "ask for the sale" and handle objections professionally.

• Tech Savviness: Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook) to track leads

• Communication: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; ability to represent the hotel brand with a professional "voice" over the phone.

• Self-Management: Must be highly organized and disciplined to work independently without direct on-site supervision.

title: Assistant Property Manager
description:
Assistant Property Manager



Job Type: Full-Time 

Work Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM Pacific Time, with a 1-hour break

Salary: $1000 per month



Job Overview: 

We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Assistant Property Manager to support the daily operations of a residential property portfolio. This remote role is responsible for assisting with maintenance coordination, tenant communication, and work order management to help ensure properties are well maintained and residents receive timely and professional service. The ideal candidate is organized, responsive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced property management environment while collaborating remotely with the management team.



Key Responsibilities:

Respond promptly and professionally to tenant inquiries and maintenance requests, ensuring timely follow-up and resolution.

Review, prioritize, and manage maintenance work orders in AppFolio.

Coordinate maintenance services using approved vendors and internal technicians.

Communicate with tenants to schedule maintenance work and provide updates on service requests, documenting communications in AppFolio.

Track maintenance requests to ensure repairs are completed accurately and on time.

Assist the property management team in obtaining repair estimates and coordinating vendor services when necessary.

Maintain accurate records, notes, and updates related to maintenance requests and tenant communications within AppFolio.

Support daily tenant correspondence and help ensure a high level of tenant satisfaction.

Assist with urgent maintenance coordination when necessary.



Required Skills & Qualifications:

Experience using property management software; AppFolio experience strongly preferred.

Strong organizational skills with excellent attention to detail.

Ability to prioritize tasks and manage time effectively in a remote work environment.

Strong problem-solving skills and sound judgment when handling maintenance or tenant concerns.

Professional written communication skills with the ability to manage tenant communications effectively.

Self-motivated, reliable, and able to remain composed in high-pressure situations.

Ability to coordinate multiple maintenance requests and follow through to completion.

Availability to assist with urgent maintenance coordination when required.





Note: An NBI or police clearance will be required upon acceptance of the job offer.

title: Remote Structural Engineer / Revit Modeler
description:
******TO BE CONSIDERED AS AN APPLICANT, PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR MOST UP-TO-DATE RESUME AND A VIDEO INTRODUCTION TAILORED TO THIS JOB POSTING!******



Remote Structural Engineer / Revit Modeler



Job Type: Full-Time

Work Hours: Sunday to Thursday, 2:00 PM-10:30 PM PST (30-minute unpaid break) - Subject to client requirements

Salary: $1,200 per month



Job Overview:

We are seeking a highly skilled Structural Engineer / Revit Modeler to join our team. This role focuses on designing and detailing multi-level storage buildings, which require a unique structural approach due to their heavy load-bearing systems and specialized construction methods.

The ideal candidate is not only a strong structural engineer but also a super expert in Revit modeling, capable of creating precise, complex models with minimal supervision.



Key Responsibilities:

Design and model heavy, multi-level storage buildings using Revit.

Perform structural analysis and detailing specific to high-load industrial and storage facilities.

Develop accurate construction documents and shop drawings.

Collaborate with the engineering and design team to ensure models meet structural and architectural requirements.

Identify and solve design challenges with a focus on constructability and safety.

Maintain accuracy and consistency across all Revit models and related documentation.

Coordinate with project managers and other disciplines to ensure seamless project delivery.



Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Structural Engineering, Civil Engineering, or related field.

Industrial and commercial engineering experience preferred, particularly with heavy load-bearing structures.

Proven expertise as a Revit Modeler, with advanced knowledge of large-scale modeling.

Strong understanding of structural systems, especially for multi-level and heavy storage buildings.

Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.

Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.

Strong communication and collaboration abilities in a remote team environment.



Preferred Skills:

Experience with Steel Structures, Concrete Structures, Masonry Construction, Light and Gauge Steel Structures

Familiarity with BIM coordination and clash detection.

Knowledge of U.S. building codes and standards is a plus.

Knowledge of ETABS and SAFE is a plus.



Note: An NBI or police clearance will be required upon acceptance of the job offer.
